title: The influence of BCG vaccine strain on mycobacteria-specific and non-specific immune responses in a prospective cohort of infants in Uganda.
ehr_evidence: None. The study is a prospective cohort study.
summary: Evaluation of specific and non-specific immune responses to different BCG vaccine strains (Russia, Bulgaria, Denmark) in a cohort of infants in Uganda.
key_findings: BCG-Denmark immunised infants showed the highest cytokine responses.
Scar frequency differed significantly by strain (Russia 52.2%, Bulgaria 64.1%, Denmark 92.6%).
Scarred infants had higher IFN-gamma and IL-13 responses to mycobacterial antigens.
BCG-Denmark group had the highest frequency of adverse events.
